children.heading: Mentored Patient-Oriented Research Career Development Awards
children.label: a Mentored Patient-Oriented Research Career Development Awards

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Director of the National Institutes of Health shall make grants (to be referred to as “Mentored Patient-Oriented Research Career Development Awards”) to support individual careers in clinical research at general clinical research centers or at other institutions that have the infrastructure and resources deemed appropriate for conducting patient-oriented clinical research.

children.heading: Use
children.label: B Use
children.text: Grants under subparagraph (A) shall be used to support clinical investigators in the early phases of their independent careers by providing salary and such other support for a period of supervised study.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: 2 Applications
children.text: An application for a grant under this subsection shall be submitted by an individual scientist at such time as the Director may require.

children.heading: Mid-Career Investigator Awards in Patient-Oriented Research
children.label: b Mid-Career Investigator Awards in Patient-Oriented Research

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Director of the National Institutes of Health shall make grants (to be referred to as “Mid-Career Investigator Awards in Patient-Oriented Research”) to support individual clinical research projects at general clinical research centers or at other institutions that have the infrastructure and resources deemed appropriate for conducting patient-oriented clinical research.

children.heading: Use
children.label: B Use
children.text: Grants under subparagraph (A) shall be used to provide support for mid-career level clinicians to allow such clinicians to devote time to clinical research and to act as mentors for beginning clinical investigators.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: 2 Applications
children.text: An application for a grant under this subsection shall be submitted by an individual scientist at such time as the Director requires.

children.heading: Graduate Training in Clinical Investigation Award
children.label: c Graduate Training in Clinical Investigation Award

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Director of the National Institutes of Health shall make grants (to be referred to as “Graduate Training in Clinical Investigation Awards”) to support individuals pursuing master’s or doctoral degrees in clinical investigation.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: 2 Applications
children.text: An application for a grant under this subsection shall be submitted by an individual scientist at such time as the Director may require.

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: 3 Limitations
children.text: Grants under this subsection shall be for terms of 2 years or more and shall provide stipend, tuition, and institutional support for individual advanced degree programs in clinical investigation.

children.label: A
children.text: Analytical methods, biostatistics, and study design.

children.label: B
children.text: Principles of clinical pharmacology and pharmacokinetics.

children.label: C
children.text: Clinical epidemiology.

children.label: D
children.text: Computer data management and medical informatics.

children.label: E
children.text: Ethical and regulatory issues.

children.label: F
children.text: Biomedical writing.

children.label: A
children.text: Analytical methods, biostatistics, and study design.

children.label: B
children.text: Principles of clinical pharmacology and pharmacokinetics.

children.label: C
children.text: Clinical epidemiology.

children.label: D
children.text: Computer data management and medical informatics.

children.label: E
children.text: Ethical and regulatory issues.

children.label: F
children.text: Biomedical writing.

children.heading: Applications
children.label: 2 Applications
children.text: An application for a grant under this subsection shall be submitted by an individual institution or a consortium of institutions at such time as the Director may require. An institution may submit only one such application.

children.heading: Limitations
children.label: 3 Limitations
children.text: Grants under this subsection shall be for terms of up to 5 years and may be renewable.
